Start update scheduling independently from Advanced Features, AuthCoordinator, and protected data pollers, and stop it on app termination.
Run a deterministic clock probe covering launch, relaunch, 24-hour cadence, sleep or wake catch-up, offline retry, and single-timer ownership.

description: Remember Later choices and re-present an available update on a controlled schedule.
steps: Add an UpdatePromptPolicy with a namespaced persistent store for deferred version and next eligible prompt date, defaulting Later to a four-hour delay.
Suppress repeated island prompts for the same version until the deferral expires while allowing manual checks in Settings at any time.
Clear stale deferral data when a newer version appears, the installed version catches up, or the user starts the update.
Run deterministic policy probes for repeated checks, relaunch, clock advance, a newer version, and successful installation state.

description: Present update prompts and downloads predictably alongside music and advanced-feature states.
steps: Add update availability and download intents to the domain reducer through an adapter owned by SceneCoordinator, keeping UpdateCoordinator free of island UI concepts.
Define update download activity as higher priority than login-required and app activity, and preserve enough return state for music takeover or normal compact mode to resume correctly.
Prevent outside clicks, hover, review or todo timers, and repeated update callbacks from corrupting an active update download presentation.
Run reducer matrices covering basic mode, advanced logged-out, advanced logged-in, music playback, available update, active download, completion, and failure.

description: Show an available update in the reusable square island shape with Update and Later actions.
steps: Reuse the login-required square shell geometry and physical spring transition while providing a separate update prompt content kind.
Render localized Update and Later buttons using Capsule backgrounds: solid blue for Update and solid gray for Later, with no border or rounded-rectangle shape.
Keep both buttons below the notch-covered center, provide accessible labels and keyboard focus, and prevent text or controls from clipping at supported sizes.
Capture deterministic prompt frames and verify button hit regions, colors, capsule geometry, notch clearance, and Reduce Motion behavior.

description: Collapse the update prompt and honor the reminder delay when the user chooses Later.
steps: Route the Later capsule action through SceneCoordinator to UpdatePromptPolicy and a reducer collapse intent without calling persistence from SwiftUI.
Collapse through the shared reverse spring and restore the correct compact or music presentation.
Verify scheduled checks before the next eligible prompt do not reopen the island and the prompt returns after the four-hour delay.
Relaunch during the delay and verify the persisted deferral remains effective while manual Settings checks still work.

description: Start a verified update download after the user chooses Update and transition cleanly out of the prompt.
steps: Route the Update capsule action from the render model to UpdateCoordinator through SceneCoordinator without exposing Sparkle commands to the view or reducer.
Collapse the square prompt first, then enter update activity when Sparkle confirms the download started, using the existing single motion driver for both transitions.
Disable duplicate Update activation and preserve the current installed app when download setup fails.
Use a signed local update fixture to verify one download begins, the prompt closes, and setup failure produces a recoverable final state.

description: Show update download progress in the native island activity shape.
steps: Add a dedicated update activity content kind that uses the existing left-and-right activity layout rather than introducing a new window or shell.
Render a continuously rotating blue progress indicator on the left and a stable-width white integer percentage on the right, leaving the notch center empty.
Keep spinner animation isolated from shell geometry and honor Reduce Motion with a nonrotating but visibly active fallback.
Render 0, 7, 42, 99, and 100 percent fixtures and verify stable dimensions, colors, notch clearance, and no layout shift.

description: Keep the island percentage synchronized with the verified update download.
steps: Convert Sparkle expected-content-length and received-byte callbacks into clamped monotonic percentage updates in UpdateCoordinator.
Handle unknown length with an indeterminate activity state and prevent regressions, division errors, or stale callbacks after retry.
Throttle presentation updates enough to avoid excessive window layout work while always delivering initial, final, and meaningful intermediate progress.
Run a controlled download stream and verify byte totals, displayed percentages, unknown-length behavior, retry reset, and final 100 percent state.

description: Install the verified update and relaunch MemoryFlow safely after download completes.
steps: Map Sparkle ready-to-install and install callbacks into UpdateCoordinator and settle the island download activity before relaunch.
Allow Sparkle to perform signature verification, atomic replacement, privileged authorization when required, and relaunch instead of implementing file replacement in app code.
Refuse installation when verification fails and keep the existing app launchable with a recoverable error exposed in Settings.
Install a signed higher-version fixture, verify the relaunched bundle version changed, and confirm the updater does not offer the same version again.

description: Recover cleanly from update network, storage, feed, and verification failures.
steps: Map offline, HTTP, malformed appcast, insufficient disk, cancelled authorization, and signature failures into user-safe UpdateCoordinator errors with diagnostic detail kept out of island copy.
Collapse failed download activity to a stable prior presentation and expose retry from Settings without silently retrying installation in a loop.
Remove temporary download state through Sparkle's lifecycle while preserving the installed application and future scheduled checks.
Run injected failure sequences and verify final presentation, retry eligibility, persisted deferral state, and absence of duplicate downloads or timers.

description: Produce a signed, notarized, and publishable MemoryFlow Island update feed for production releases.
steps: Add a release command that archives with monotonically increasing marketing and build versions, signs with Developer ID, submits for notarization, staples the ticket, and packages the app for Sparkle.
Generate an EdDSA-signed appcast entry over HTTPS with minimum-system-version, release notes, content length, and phased-rollout metadata while keeping the private signing key outside the repository.
Publish the update artifact before atomically publishing the appcast so clients never receive a feed entry whose download is unavailable.
Verify Gatekeeper assessment, notarization status, appcast signature, downgrade rejection, minimum macOS filtering, and staged-rollout eligibility using a release candidate fixture.

description: Prove update discovery, deferral, download progress, and installation work without login in a production-like release path.
steps: Launch a signed older build with Advanced Features disabled and verify scheduled and manual checks both discover the signed newer release without authentication.
Choose Later, verify the square prompt collapses and remains deferred across relaunch, then advance the controlled schedule and verify the prompt returns.
Choose Update and verify square collapse, blue left spinner, white right percentage, signed download, safe installation, and relaunch into the newer version.

Record appcast, signing, state-transition, percentage, visual-frame, installed-version, and failure-recovery evidence under docs/evidence/mac-island-updater without modifying Windows or Electron behavior.

description: Install verified updates safely and recover without damaging the current app when updating fails.
steps: Map Sparkle ready, verification, authorization, installation, and relaunch callbacks into UpdateCoordinator and settle the island activity before replacement or relaunch.
Delegate EdDSA verification, temporary-file lifecycle, privileged authorization, atomic replacement, and relaunch to Sparkle instead of implementing app replacement in MemoryFlow code.

Map offline, HTTP, malformed feed, insufficient disk, cancelled authorization, and signature errors into recoverable state, collapse to a stable presentation, and expose retry from Settings without an automatic loop.
Install a signed higher-version fixture and verify relaunch, changed bundle version, no repeated offer, and preserved app launchability after each injected failure.
Verify temporary state, timers, prompt deferral, duplicate operations, and future scheduled checks are correct after success and failure.

description: Publish signed and notarized MemoryFlow Island releases through a secure staged appcast pipeline.
steps: Add a release command that enforces increasing marketing and build versions, archives with Developer ID, submits for notarization, staples the ticket, and packages the app for Sparkle.

Generate an EdDSA-signed HTTPS appcast entry with minimum macOS, release notes, content length, and phased-rollout metadata while keeping credentials and the private update key outside the repository.
Publish the immutable update artifact before atomically publishing the appcast so clients never discover an unavailable download.
Verify Gatekeeper, notarization, appcast signature, downgrade rejection, minimum-system filtering, staged-rollout eligibility, and artifact availability with a release candidate.
Document the release inputs, secret locations, rollback procedure, and evidence commands without placing secrets in generated logs.

description: Prove the complete basic, advanced, login-required, and production update experience on macOS.
steps: Launch with Advanced Features disabled and no session, verify an empty compact notch center, no protected traffic, working music, and login-independent manual and scheduled update checks.

Enable Advanced Features, verify the Login Required square and Settings browser login path, restore authenticated review and todo behavior, then disable advanced mode without deleting the saved session.
Discover a signed newer build, verify Later across relaunch, then choose Update and verify prompt collapse, blue spinner, white percentage, safe installation, and relaunch into the new version.

Exercise invalid signature, offline download, insufficient disk, authorization cancellation, music takeover, sleep or wake, rapid clicks, and app relaunch without duplicate timers or corrupted presentation state.
Record API, appcast, signing, transition, visual, percentage, installed-version, and failure evidence under docs/evidence/mac-island-phase7 without modifying Windows or Electron behavior.

description: Install an EdDSA-verified unsigned GitHub release safely and recover without damaging the current app.
steps: Finish the existing Sparkle verification, ready, installation, relaunch, recoverable failure, and explicit retry lifecycle without requiring Developer ID, notarization, or a Team ID.

Build real older and higher-version app bundles with ad-hoc signing, package the higher build as a Sparkle archive, sign it with a test-only EdDSA key outside tracked files, and serve the appcast and archive over controlled HTTPS.

Install the higher build through Sparkle after the user-approved unsigned-app launch path, then verify changed CFBundleVersion, relaunch, no repeated offer, and continued launchability after injected failures.

Verify offline, HTTP, malformed feed, insufficient disk, cancelled installation, invalid EdDSA signature, duplicate operations, stale callbacks, timers, deferral, and future checks without an automatic retry loop.

description: Package and publish unsigned MemoryFlow Island releases through GitHub Releases and a signed Sparkle appcast.
steps: Add a release command and GitHub Actions workflow that enforce increasing marketing and build versions, build the macOS app, apply ad-hoc signing, package the app for Sparkle, and never request Developer ID or notarization credentials.

Generate an EdDSA-signed appcast entry with GitHub Release HTTPS URLs, minimum macOS, release notes, content length, checksum, and phased-rollout metadata while keeping the private update key in a local or GitHub Actions secret.
Upload the immutable archive and checksums before publishing the appcast or release metadata so clients never discover a missing asset.

Validate the workflow locally or with a release-candidate run, including appcast signature, archive download, downgrade rejection, minimum-system filtering, version monotonicity, and absence of private keys in artifacts or logs.

description: Prove the complete open-source macOS experience using unsigned GitHub releases and EdDSA-verified updates.
steps: Document and verify first installation from a GitHub Release, including drag-to-Applications and the macOS right-click Open or Privacy and Security approval path, with a clear warning that the app is not notarized.
Verify basic mode, music, login-independent checks, Advanced Features, the Login Required square, browser login, review, todo, and session preservation without changing Windows or Electron behavior.

Discover the higher GitHub-style release, verify Later across relaunch, then verify prompt collapse, blue spinner, white percentage, EdDSA verification, replacement, and relaunch into the new version.

Exercise invalid signature, offline download, insufficient disk, cancelled installation, sleep or wake, rapid clicks, and relaunch, then record install guidance, appcast, transition, visual, percentage, installed-version, and recovery evidence under docs/evidence/mac-island-phase7.

description: Prove the complete open-source macOS experience from GitHub installation through unsigned Sparkle updating.
steps: Document and verify installation from a GitHub Release, including drag-to-Applications and macOS right-click Open or Privacy and Security approval, with a clear warning that the app is not notarized.

Verify basic mode, music, login-independent checks, Advanced Features, the Login Required square, browser login, review, todo, and saved-session preservation without changing Windows or Electron behavior.
Use a draft or prerelease candidate to verify Later across relaunch, prompt collapse, blue spinner, white percentage, EdDSA verification, app replacement, and relaunch into the higher version.

Exercise invalid signature, offline download, insufficient disk, cancelled installation, sleep or wake, rapid clicks, and relaunch, then record CI, release asset, install guidance, appcast, transition, visual, percentage, installed-version, and recovery evidence under docs/evidence/mac-island-phase7.
